2026-W28.lede: This week the humanoid gold rush produced a small army of new contenders — companion bots, IPOs, and a Tesla defector's European venture — while Waymo quietly went about the less glamorous business of becoming boring, rolling into six more American cities like a chain restaurant expanding one strip mall at a time. Meanwhile, drones kept doing what humanoids only promise: showing up, doing the job, and going home.
